pageTitle.value: Felix Brooks-Church's Sanku - Perpetual Planet - Video

pageCaption.value: Social entrepreneur Felix Brooks-church’s mission is to eliminate malnutrition. His “dosifier”, a machine that fortifies flour with essential nutrients at minimal cost, reaches more than 25 million people across East Africa.

WaPageTitle.value: Sanku de Felix Brooks-Church - Perpetual Planet

metadescription.value: With Rolex's support, Felix Brooks-Church and his non-profit Sanku are expanding operations massively into Ethiopia. Watch the video on rolex.org.

text.value: Social entrepreneur Felix Brooks-church has improved millions of lives in East Africa by fortifying staple foods with essential nutrients at minimal costs. As part of our Perpetual Planet Initiative, he is expanding his work into Ethiopia. By launching operations into this country – a far larger region than he has worked in before – the Rolex Awards Laureate has taken a significant step towards ending malnutrition.

pageTitle.value: Grégoire Courtine's Life in motion - Perpetual Planet - Video

pageCaption.value: Neuroscientist Grégoire Courtine's revolutionary "digital bridge" technology could change the lives of those with spinal cord injuries. Learn more on rolex.org.

WaPageTitle.value: Grégoire Courtine's Life in motion - Perpetual Planet - Video

metadescription.value: Neuroscientist Grégoire Courtine's revolutionary "digital bridge" technology could change the lives of those with spinal cord injuries. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: Piyush Tewari is determined to save thousands of lives by ensuring that most accident victims in Delhi receive rapid medical care. His foundation, in partnership with hospitals and a medical science institute, has already provided training sessions in basic life support to over 2,000 police officers, as well as 500 ordinary citizens.

pageCaption.value: Billy Lee Lasley’s invention of a non-invasive method for determining the sex of birds — designed to help protect endangered species such as eagles — has slowly been adopted by researchers around the world. Perhaps more importantly, his work has proved to have other uses, including monitoring the reproductive health of women exposed to chemicals in the environment.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Securing Nigeria's food supply - Video

pageCaption.value: In 2010, Nnaemeka Ikegwuonu, a Nigerian social entrepreneur, won a Rolex Award for Enterprise for creating a radio station to advise smallholder farmers.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Securing Nigeria's food supply - Video

metadescription.value: Rolex Awards Laureate Nnaemeka Ikegwuonu is developing projects to strengthen Nigeria's food systems and eliminate wastage. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Software that cuts food waste - Video

pageCaption.value: Oscar Ekponimo, of Nigeria, is solving an old problem –  malnutrition caused by poverty – with a 21st century invention. His cloud-based app, Chowberry, alerts retailers when products reach the end of shelf life. Discounted food can then be offered to relief agencies, cutting waste. Ekponimo, who suffered from hunger as a child, developed his app to help the millions of Nigerians who do not get enough to eat.

pageTitle.value: Cool food in the desert - Video

pageCaption.value: Nigerian teacher Mohammed Bah Abba was motivated by his concern for the rural poor and by his interest in indigenous African technology to seek a practical, local solution to these problems. His extremely simple and inexpensive earthenware "pot-in-pot" cooling device, based on a principle of physics already known in ancient Egypt, has revolutionized lives in this semi-desert area.
